top_posts.author: Unknown
top_posts.title: Hospitality News Middle East's Post - LinkedIn
top_posts.preview: The Saudi Red Sea Authority has appointed Dr Maryam Ficociello as CEO, effective March 22, 2026, marking a new leadership phase.
top_posts.draft_comment: The governance-resilience intersection is where coastal authorities either gain stakeholder trust or lose it fast. Twenty years spanning public and private compliance work gives Dr Ficociello exactly the toolkit needed for marine tourism regulation. Curious how the authority plans to balance environmental targets with the speed of Red Sea giga-project timelines.

top_posts.author: Unknown
top_posts.title: #ai #robotics #aigovernance | AI for Good - LinkedIn
top_posts.preview: Our #AI and #Robotics Programme Officer Guillem Martínez Roura is heading to Riyadh to speak at the 3rd Global AI Summit (GAIN) taking place
top_posts.draft_comment: Ran the AI governance gauntlet across three MENA regulatory bodies last year and the fragmentation problem is real. GAIN's cross-vendor roundtable format is the right setup because no single hyperscaler framework scales across borders. Looking for what Saudi's approach produces that EU AI Act discussions have not.

top_posts.author: Unknown
top_posts.title: Maryam Ficociello appointed CEO of Saudi Red Sea Authority
top_posts.preview: Maryam Ali Ficociello as chief executive officer, effective March 22, 2026. The authority said the appointment supports its mandate to develop
top_posts.draft_comment: Coastal governance authority mandates are among the hardest to execute because they cut across tourism, environment, and maritime law simultaneously. The 20-year compliance track record is the right foundation for this mandate. How will the authority sequence the regulatory framework relative to the giga-project build-out pace?

top_posts.author: Unknown
top_posts.title: Saudi Red Sea Authority names Dr Maryam Ali Ficociello as CEO
top_posts.preview: #Industry | The Saudi Red Sea Authority has appointed Dr Maryam Ali Ficociello as CEO, effective March 22, 2026. Bringing more than 20 years
top_posts.draft_comment: Saudi Red Sea Authority regulating marine tourism and coastal protection under one mandate is a governance design challenge most jurisdictions split across three ministries. Twenty years of risk and resilience work gives her the operational credibility to hold that together. Which regulatory gap is she likely to address first?

top_posts.title: Santosh Kotnis' Post - LinkedIn
top_posts.preview: Santosh Kotnis. CEO | Building SpringUp | Driving AI-Enabled IT Services, BOT-Based Global Delivery & Scalable GCC ... Industry 4.0 : Disruptive
top_posts.draft_comment: The emerging leader segment at these conferences consistently generates the sharpest questions because they have fresh frameworks but no institutional blinders. What specific challenge are you bringing to the table for peer debate, rather than just presenting?
